summary.intent: Implement Task #286 â€” the framework-agnostic Table state machine for ars-ui, covering row/column selection, sort, expansion, grid focus, column resize, and virtual scrolling ARIA metadata, with all spec drifts fixed in the same PR.
summary.outcome: Full Table core implemented (~1843 LOC mod.rs, ~2000 LOC tests.rs, 37 insta snapshots, 113 passing tests), two rounds of Codex review addressed (14 total findings), rebased on main, force-pushed to PR #651, all threads resolved and Codex pass 3 triggered.

repo: Tabs (`crates/ars-components/src/navigation/tabs/mod.rs`) is the canonical structural template for complex stateful components â€” same section order (State â†’ Event â†’ Messages â†’ Props â†’ Context â†’ Part â†’ Machine â†’ Api) must be followed.
CLAUDE.md mandates that spec/code mismatches are resolved in the same PR as the code; spec edits must land alongside implementation.
`cargo xci-fast` is the 10-gate pre-push gate; it must pass (including clippy zero-warnings, coverage â‰Ą 90%, insta --unreferenced=reject) before any push.
Proptests are marked `#[ignore = "proptest â€” nightly extended-proptest job"]` by workspace convention.
Post-implementation-audit (spec/impl drift + iterative missing-anything + test-coverage) is mandatory per CLAUDE.md step 7 before user review.
Codex review loop is mandatory: post `@codex review`, poll until terminal state, address all findings with TDD regression tests, resolve threads via GraphQL, re-trigger â€” repeat until đź‘Ť.
Snapshot fixtures require the `snapshot-reviewed` label on any PR that creates `.snap` files.
Coverage threshold is 90% (both line and branch) enforced by `cargo xci-fast`; the table module ended at ~92.7% lines / 91.4% branches.

The `select_all_attrs` pass-all-row-keys idiom: `ConnectApi::part_attrs` must route `Part::SelectAllCheckbox` through the actual `ctx.rows` slice, not an empty `&[]` â€” otherwise `aria-checked` always reports no selection.

`selection::Set::All` is not materialized by `selection::State::deselect/toggle`; callers must detect `Set::All` and manually build `Set::Multiple(ctx.rows \ {key})` when individual rows are toggled off after a SelectAll.

Controlled Bindable sync events must use `Option<Option<T>>` semantics: outer `None` exits controlled mode, outer `Some(None)` sets controlled value to None, outer `Some(Some(v))` sets controlled value to v â€” using `Option<T>` prevents the adapter from ever reverting to uncontrolled.

`on_props_changed` must be overridden in `ConnectApi` to dispatch `SyncProps` / `SyncControlled*` / `SetDirection` events; without the override, no controlled-mode prop changes are ever dispatched to the machine.
`SortColumn` must NOT set `is_sorting = true` internally â€” that flag is adapter-controlled lifecycle state (async load), surfaced via a dedicated `SetIsSorting(bool)` event.
`SelectAll` semantics differ by mode: `SelectAllMode::AllVisible` materializes `Set::Multiple(ctx.rows)` (visible page only), `SelectAllMode::AllData` writes `Set::All` (server-side select-all).

code.finding: Machine::transition signature uses `_props` parameter; must rename to `props` when SyncProps arm reads from it â€” otherwise the compiler silently ignores all prop-to-context field copies.

context_only closure parameter types must be explicitly annotated (e.g., `|ctx: &mut Context|`) â€” the compiler can't infer them from the SortDescriptor generic without the annotation, causing E0282.
SetRows must rebase focused_cell.1 (row_index) in addition to dropping stale row keys from focus â€” row reorder/sort/filter shifts indices even when the key is still present.
SyncProps must re-sync ComponentIds-derived `id` and `caption_id` when props.id changes; they are not automatically re-derived by the machine framework.
Â§1.7 FocusCell had a literal bug hardcoding row index 0; correct payload is `{ row: Key, col: usize, row_index: usize }` and the transition writes `(col, row_index)`.
Â§3.4.2: Non-sortable columns MUST omit `aria-sort` entirely â€” the spec's example was setting `aria-sort="none"` unconditionally which violates ARIA 1.2.
Rebase conflict: main added a `layout` module here while the branch added `data_display`; both must be present after resolution.

workflow: TDD is strictly enforced: write failing tests (compile error counts as red) before touching implementation â€” Codex findings were all addressed test-first.
Codex thread replies must cite the specific commit SHA and name the regression test that locks in the fix â€” generic replies are insufficient for thread resolution.
All Codex threads must be resolved via GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation after addressing; just commenting is not enough.
Rebase (not merge) against main before force-push to keep PR commit history linear; conflicts must be resolved then staged before `git rebase --continue`.
Coverage improvement is a good signal: adding regression tests for Codex findings raised branch coverage from 87.95% to 91.40%.
`cargo insta test --features i18n --unreferenced=reject` is the correct command to verify no orphaned snapshot fixtures remain after test reorganization.
When an API error kills the plan mid-write, the user may not see the partial output â€” the next action should be to write the plan to disk immediately rather than re-deriving it from context.

friction: API socket error mid-plan-writing caused the plan to be lost; had to write it to disk in a follow-up turn, which confused the user ('Where is the plan?').
Two full Codex review rounds (8 findings + 6 findings = 14 total) required before approval, each needing a full TDD + rebase + force-push + thread-resolve cycle.

The `selection::Set::All` materialization bug was subtle â€” the `state.deselect()` method silently no-ops on `Set::All`, so SelectAll followed by ToggleRow appeared to work but left state inconsistent. Not detectable by static analysis.
`SyncControlledSortDescriptor` payload semantics mismatch (`Option<T>` vs `Option<Option<T>>`) required updating both the Event enum signature and all call sites in tests and on_props_changed.

The `on_props_changed` omission (no override) caused controlled-mode sync to be completely silently broken â€” no compile error, no test failure until Codex specifically constructed the multi-render scenario.
Clippy `unused-mut` warning in the round-2 regression tests blocked `xci-fast` and required an extra fix+push cycle.
One rebase conflict on `spec_conformance.rs` required manual resolution (main added `layout` module while branch added `data_display`).
Background Codex poller task output had to be manually read via file path â€” there was no structured way to get a summary of findings without parsing raw GitHub API JSON.
open_items: `ScrollToRow(Key)` event and full Virtualizer integration explicitly deferred â€” would require widening `type Effect` from `NoEffect` to a typed enum and pulling in `ars-collections::Virtualizer` plumbing.
`BulkSelection` exclusion-set wrapper deferred â€” described as adapter/application code that wraps `selection::Set::All`; not part of the agnostic core.
`on_load_more: Option<Callback<()>>` callback is adapter-only and not stored in the core machine.
Codex pass 3 was triggered at end of session but result not yet known â€” PR #651 may have additional findings.
Mutation testing shard run (195 mutants, 8 shards) was started but only shard 0 was evaluated due to the ~30 min full-run cost; mutation coverage of the transitions is unverified beyond the shard.
